label: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 12

extraction.one_line_summary: CORT Magnus won from the peloton sprint after earlier break caught inside 5km, outsprinting BAGIOLI and MATTHEWS with perfect GREGAARD leadout
tactical_patterns: CORT Magnus conserved energy by skipping breakaway after consecutive days racing, then positioned perfectly in reduced bunch sprint after selective climbs
LASTRA Jonathan attacked first on final climb creating quartet with CICCONE, VINE, HENAO, BARDET forcing peloton to chase and fracture sprinter teams
Alto San Jerónimo crash scattered ROGLIČ and YATES support riders, forcing tempo-making that later reduced field for sprint finish
BARDET Romain attacked polka-dot jersey aggressively despite only 4-point deficit to CARUSO, signaling confidence in climbing form beyond GC ambitions

rider_signals.CORT Magnus: positive: demonstrated tactical maturity by avoiding third consecutive breakaway then still winning reduced sprint, proving versatility across stage profiles
rider_signals.BAGIOLI Andrea: positive: executed solo sprint without leadout train to finish second against faster sprinters, showing punch on selective terrain
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: BikeExchange positioning failed to deliver Matthews into podium position despite selective finish suiting his profile over pure sprinters
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: positive: attacked for KOM points despite small deficit to CARUSO, showing climbing legs are peaking after earlier Vuelta struggles
rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: made selection in late breakaway quartet on punchy finish despite being caught, signaling emerging form for hillier stages
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: stomach issues causing 10+ minute losses confirm race collapse for 2015 champion, signaling career decline trajectory
